name: Ruta de Valdeganga - Las Mariquillas - Bolinches
name_original: Las Mariquillas desde Valdeganga — circular por la orilla del Júcar
description: Cycle the moderate 11.2-mile Las Mariquillas from Valdeganga circular route along the Júcar River, featuring diverse terrain and scenic view
region_intro: Touring cycling around Valdeganga offers exploration of a picturesque municipality in Spain, characterized by the Júcar River and its carved gorge. The region features a rich river valley, extensive vineyards, and riverine parks, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Located less than 30 kilometers northeast of Albacete, the area includes both flat plains and more undulating sections along the riverbanks. Best touring cycling routes around Valdeganga The most popular touring cycling route is Casas de Juan Núñez, Jorquera y el río Júcar — circular desde Valdeganga, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Júcar River and passes through the historic town of Jorquera. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ruta de Valdeganga - Las Mariquillas - Bolinches, a moderate 11.2 miles (18.0 km) path. This route offers a mix of terrain, including sections near Las Mariquillas and Bolinches. Local touring cyclists also love the View of the Júcar – Alcalá del Júcar loop from Bormate, a 40.1 miles (64.6 km) trail leading through the Júcar River valley, often completed in about 4 hours 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Valdeganga is defined by the Júcar River valley, vineyards, and riverine parks. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Valdeganga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valdeganga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Valdeganga?
A: Valdeganga offers a diverse network of over 20 touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy, leisurely rides to more challenging, difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Valdeganga?
A: The terrain around Valdeganga is quite varied, shaped significantly by the Júcar River. You can expect routes that follow the river valley, offering lush riverside environments and panoramic views. There are also sections that traverse abundant vineyards and more undulating areas, providing a mix of flat plains and gentle climbs. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380344423/ruta-de-valdeganga-las-mariquillas-bolinches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de Valdeganga - Las Mariquillas - Bolinches&lt;/a&gt;, offer a mix of terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly touring cycling routes in Valdeganga?
A: Yes, Valdeganga offers a few easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. The region is known for dedicated cycling paths, such as the one connecting Albacete to Valdeganga, which is paved, traffic-free, and in excellent condition. While specific easy routes are not detailed in the provided data, the overall network includes options suitable for less experienced cyclists.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Valdeganga?
A: Absolutely. Many of Valdeganga's touring cycling routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380360544/casas-de-juan-nunez-jorquera-y-el-rio-jucar-circular-desde-valdeganga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casas de Juan Núñez, Jorquera y el río Júcar — circular desde Valdeganga&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Júcar River and passes through the historic town of Jorquera. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Square – Jorquera River Pool loop from Valdeganga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Valdeganga's routes are rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You'll frequently encounter the stunning Júcar River and its gorge, lush vineyards, and riverine parks. Notable landmarks include the ruins of the 12th-century Gothic hermitage of San Benito (El Santo) and Puente Torres, the oldest bridge in the municipality, possibly of Roman origin. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas on the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Valdeganga is home to several excellent viewpoints. The "View of the Júcar" is a popular spot offering wonderful panoramic vistas of the river valley. Many routes along the Júcar River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage – Views of the Júcar River loop from Valdeganga&lt;/a&gt;, provide magnificent views, especially along the section between Recueja and Jorquera.

Q: Is Valdeganga suitable for family cycling trips?
A: Valdeganga can be suitable for family cycling, especially on its dedicated, paved, and traffic-free cycling paths. The fluvial park "Puentes de Valdeganga" offers a recreational area by the river, which can be a good starting point for easier, flatter paths. For families with younger children, it's advisable to choose routes with minimal elevation gain and away from main roads.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the touring cycling routes in Valdeganga?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the natural trails and paths in Valdeganga, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always best to check local regulations for specific areas or protected natural parks. The riverside trails offer pleasant environments for cycling with a canine companion.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a cycling tour in Valdeganga?
A: You'll find convenient parking options in Valdeganga. The fluvial park "Puentes de Valdeganga" serves as a recreational area right by the river and is a good starting point for many routes, likely offering parking. Additionally, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2826788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Mariquillas&lt;/a&gt; is a known parking area that acts as an access point for trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Valdeganga?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally the most pleasant for touring cycling in Valdeganga. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be quite hot, especially in July and August, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for cycling on clear days.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Valdeganga?
A: The touring cycling routes in Valdeganga are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning views of the Júcar River valley, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any historical sites I can visit while cycling?
A: Yes, Valdeganga's routes often intertwine with its rich history. Cyclists can encounter the ruins of the 12th-century Gothic hermitage of San Benito (also known as El Santo) and Puente Torres, which features the oldest bridge in the municipality, possibly of Roman origin. The area also showcases ancient irrigation systems of Muslim origin, adding a historical dimension to your ride.

Q: Are there any routes that are mostly paved or traffic-free?
A: Yes, Valdeganga offers dedicated cycling paths that are paved and traffic-free, making for a very pleasant and safe cycling experience. A notable example is the path connecting Albacete to Valdeganga, which is in excellent condition and ideal for leisurely rides away from vehicle traffic.

Q: Can I access Valdeganga's cycling routes using public transport?
A: Valdeganga is less than 30 kilometers northeast of Albacete, which is a larger transport hub. While specific public transport links directly to trailheads are not detailed, you can likely reach Valdeganga itself via public transport from Albacete, and then access routes from within the municipality. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections.
tips: Especially in summer, when you are away for a longer period of time, it is valuable to know where appropriate shady spots can be found.

Located on the right bank of the Júcar River, the town is surrounded by countless green routes along which Roman roads once passed. It is worth seeing the Church of the Purísima Concepción, the Roman bridge, the El Santo Natural Park and Alcalá del Júcar.
Nice dirt road between Las Mariquillas and Valdeganga, which passes along the bank of the Júcar River. Enjoy the shade and head to the water to wet your feet and cool off on a hot day.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route conveniently starts near a parking lot in Valdeganga. This makes it easy to access the trail and begin your cycling adventure along the Júcar River.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mixed surface. You'll find approximately 8.64 km of unpaved sections, 7.29 km of asphalt, and 1.94 km of paved surfaces. Expect a "nice dirt road" along the Júcar River between Las Mariquillas and Valdeganga, and some areas where the Júcar River has carved out ravines.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers diverse scenery and points of interest. You'll cycle along the right bank of the Júcar River, passing through lush riverbank vegetation, riverside forests, and agricultural areas with vineyards and orchards. Notable features include the "Charco Azul" (Blue Pond), remnants of a Roman bridge, old Roman roads, and the "Piscifactoría de Bolinches" (Bolinches fish farm). Don't miss the Riverside recreation area for a break.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mixed terrain and riverside setting, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery, including the poplars, holm oaks, and vineyards, is particularly vibrant or colorful. Summers can be hot, and some unpaved sections might be muddy after heavy rain.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this as a "moderate" bike ride requiring "Good fitness." While some sources suggest shorter sections have "no technical difficulty" and "practically no elevation gain," the full 18 km route includes unpaved sections that can be more challenging. It's best suited for cyclists with some experience and good fitness, rather than absolute beginners or very young children.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, there are no specific restrictions mentioned regarding dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to clean up after them. Be mindful of other cyclists and pedestrians.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Las Mariquillas from Valdeganga route. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area is ecologically rich, offering chances to spot diverse wildlife. You might see protected birds like Bonelli's eagle and peregrine falcon, especially in the "Hoz del Júcar" (Júcar Gorge). Mammals such as mountain goats and otters also inhabit the riverbanks and surrounding areas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find yourself on sections of the Camino de Las Mariquillas a Valdeganga, Camino de Valdeganga a Bolinches, and the Sendero del Rio Jucar, among others. This offers a glimpse into the network of routes in the area.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or rest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are several recreational areas along the Júcar River, including Las Mariquillas and Bolinches-El Charco Azul, which are ideal for rest. The well-regarded Las Mariquillas restaurant, offering traditional local dishes by the river, is a popular spot for touring cyclists to refuel.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle route, pack essentials like water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit, and appropriate cycling attire. Given the mixed terrain, sturdy tires are advisable. Sun protection (sunscreen, hat) is important, and a light jacket might be useful for cooler parts of the day or if you stop for a break by the river.

waypoints.name: Path from Las Mariquillas to Valdeganga
